title: Supplementary code for digital right to work checks (0.4)
body: "0. Version and certification validity notes  0.a. This version of the 0.4 publication of the supplementary code for digital right to work digital identity checks (‘the RtW supplementary code’) comes into force on 1 December 2025 and is the first statutory RtW supplementary code under section 29 of the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (‘the Act’).    0.b. The certifiable rules previously set out in Home Office regulations and guidance for employers, most recently published on 12 February 2025, and the non-statutory 0.4 version of the RtW supplementary code published on 26 June 2025 will remain valid for a limited period of time (see 0.c.) for the purposes of:     certification, such that certificates issued against those versions are valid and any audit and surveillance in respect of those certificates are conducted against those versions as relevant; and     Part 2 of the Act (where relevant and where there exist certificates which are valid against them) such that references to supplementary code are to be read as references to the certifiable rules set out in Home Office regulations and guidance for employers, and to the non-statutory 0.4 version of the RtW supplementary code where those versions apply.      0.c These non-statutory versions will no longer apply for the purposes of certification and the Act, and certificates issued against them will expire and should be ignored for those purposes from and including the following dates respectively:      certificates against the certifiable rules previously set out in Home Office regulations and guidance for employers, most recently published on 12 February 2025, will expire on 31 March 2026; and    certificates against the non-statutory 0.4 version of the RtW supplementary code will expire on the date set out in the next version of the statutory RtW supplementary code as to when certifications against the 0.4 version of the RtW supplementary code expire.      0.d For clarity, the content in this statutory version of the 0.4 RtW supplementary code is entirely unchanged from the 0.4 non-statutory version save for the replacement of an earlier validity statement by 0.a-0.d in this section 0. The non-statutory publication can be found on the UK Government Web Archive. Certificates against both versions accordingly confirm compliance with the same rules.    0.e. The RtW supplementary code sets out rules for digital verification services (DVS) conducting right to work checks on holders of British and Irish passports or passport cards. It is published by the Office for Digital Identities and Attributes (OfDIA), part of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT). OfDIA is responsible for maintenance of the supplementary code.  0.f. DVS have been able to undergo certification for this purpose since 6 April 2022 as part of the UK digital identity and attributes trust framework certification scheme, by being certified against rules previously set out in Home Office regulations and guidance for employers. The most recent version of the Home Office employer’s guide to right to work checks was published on 12 February 2025 and can be accessed on the UK Government Web Archive.  0.g. This publication is designed to support continuity, and...
